key_insight: Glassy polymers yield and neck because stretching increases molecular mobility, causing a transition from shear-frozen to shear-unfrozen behavior.
engineering_relevance: The theory offers a simple constitutive framework to predict yield stress and neck initiation across strain rate and temperature for polymer forming design.
method: Uses a Doolittle volume-dependent relaxation time to model activated mobility.
Derives constitutive equations from the Onsager variational principle.
Predicts analytical yield-stress scaling and a necking phase diagram under constant strain rate.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports analytical predictions for yielding stress and a phase diagram for necking initiation, but does not mention experiments or numerical validation.
watch_item: The model is intentionally minimal, so its predictive accuracy may depend on how well the assumed relaxation-time law captures a specific polymer and loading condition.
paper_title: Shear Unfreezing Explains Yielding, Plasticity and Neck Initiation of Glassy Polymers
